experiences.title: Data Science Intern
bullets: Building ETL pipelines to process and prepare real estate data for modeling and analysis
Gaining hands-on experience with Python, scikit-learn, and data visualization while collaborating cross-functionally in a proptech environment
Creating predictive models (XGBoost) for real estate pricing by exploring and preprocessing property datasets, training and evaluating machine learning models

experiences.title: Economic Research Assistant
bullets: Built reproducible ETL pipelines to source, aggregate, and clean large, multi-source datasets (UNHCR, UMPS, USCIS) using Python, R, and Stata, while documenting said pipelines
Conducted Exploratory Data Analysis on refugee presence and crop prices in Sub-Saharan Africa (QGIS, Stata) as well as demographic impact on cohabitation across the US (R, Python)
Used Regression and DiD Analysis (Stata) to segment farming populations in Africa and determine if refugee presence increased crop prices

Utilized Time-Series and Breakpoint Analysis (R, Python) to identify statistically-significant structural changes in cohabitation behavior in the US from 2007 to 2024 in major metropolitan areas, while using regression analysis to determine the most significant demographic factors in these changes

projects.name: California Real Estate Price Prediction(Stacked Ensemble Model)
bullets: Built an end-to-end ML pipeline to predict single family home sales prices across California using on and off-market MLS sold data(100K+ observations, performing geospatial filtering, applying missingness mechanism analysis, and creating custom imputation strategies. Engineered polynomial/interaction features, applied feature targeting, and implemented geo-clustering, to capture non-linear, high-cardinality, and spatial pricing patterns. Developed a 2-level stacked ensemble(XGBoost+LIghtGBM) with GBRegressor meta-learner using segment-aware weighting and RandomizedSearchCV, achieving sub-8% Median Absolute Percent Error, comparable to Redfin’s model.
